feature/issue-58-integrate-coderlm-adapt.task_description: Add a second status line to nf-statusline.js showing tool availability: '● coderlm │ · River │ · embed' printed on a new line below the main statusline
feature/issue-58-integrate-coderlm-adapt.approach: Modify hooks/nf-statusline.js to add a new function buildToolsStatusLine() that detects and formats the three tools (coderlm, River, embed) with proper ● (bright) and · (dim) indicators. For coderlm: check if binary exists at ~/.claude/nf-bin/coderlm (· dim), then check if PID in ~/.claude/nf-bin/coderlm.pid is alive via process.kill(pid, 0) (● green if alive, omit if binary missing). For River: always shown as built-in — check for .nf-river-state.json in workspace dir, use · dim if absent or ● with color (green for active/post-explore, cyan for exploring, yellow for shadow) if present, leveraging existing qTable and lastShadow logic. For embed: check if node_modules/@huggingface/transformers exists (· dim if found, omit if missing, since no runtime signal exists). Build the tools line as a second process.stdout.write() call with leading newline, assembling only the installed/active tools. Copy hooks/nf-statusline.js to hooks/dist/nf-statusline.js, then run node bin/install.js --claude --global.
out_of_scope: Modifying River ML algorithm logic or exploration thresholds
Adding new tool detection beyond coderlm, River, and embed
Changing the main statusline format or order
Adding configuration options for tool visibility
Creating new test files beyond updating existing assertions
feature/issue-58-integrate-coderlm-adapt.risk_reason: Hook files in hooks/dist/ must be synced as part of this change, which is a guardrail trigger; installer-adjacent changes combined with multiple file modifications across the statusline system elevate this to high risk.
